prompt: Invent an original, vivid metaphor for the experience of learning a new language as an adult. Explain it in three sentences.
response: Learning a new language as an adult is like stepping into a vast, living cathedral built entirely of sound—each word a stained-glass window you must learn to see through without shattering. At first, you grope in dimness, your tongue a blunt tool tapping hollow echoes, but slowly, syllables begin to catch color and cast patterns you can walk through. One day a whole sentence lights up, and for a breath you stand inside fluent sunbeams, both foreign and home.
